_about: 224XL T&C/ARU/DMEM/FPC timing, transcribed from Service Manual figs 3.2-3.6. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: render_timing.py emits both the markdown spec and round-trip waveform PNGs from this file. Quantized to the discrete grid of each figure (MS-state, AS-state, or sample-step) so there is no continuous-time ambiguity; nanosecond figures are kept only as annotations. wave tokens: 0=low 1=high .=hold-prev p=clock-pulse ==valid/data-box(label from data[]) x=invalid/unspecified z=hi-Z. conf: high|med|low.

figures.title: T&C module timing (clock skeleton)
figures.grid_note: One microinstruction = 292.97 ns = MS0..MS8 (32.55 ns each). IMPORTANT: the top *bracket* AS0=MS0-2/AS1=MS3-5/AS2=MS6-8 labels the conceptual ARU-state THIRDS (= fig-3.4 'ARU STATE'); the AS0/AS1/AS2 *signal* rows below are the actual flip-flop strobes, which are CLOCKED BY MS EDGES and therefore sit ~1 MS LATER than their thirds (AS1 is also deliberately narrowed). The true state boundaries are delimited by ARU CK (rises ~MS0.3/3.3/6.3). Per §3.5: MC=30.72 MHz, PLL ×15 from the 2.048 MHz 02/ SBC clock (MC4044 U27 + ÷2 JK U26; tank 61.44 MHz); state gen = ÷9 counter U56 + 8-bit shift register U39 (MS0-MS8); AS grouping by U38/U25/U23. Edge positions below verified by pixel-measuring fig-3.2 against an MC-calibrated MS grid (90 px/MS). Sub-MS rise/fall = clk-to-Q lag (the <17/<19/<30 ns annotations); the HIGH cells are integer-MS.

signals.name: MC
signals.note: MC=30.72 MHz, period 32.55 ns = exactly one MS, so 1 pulse per MS (§3.5)

signals.name: O2
signals.value_source: 02/ = SBC system clock 2.048 MHz (488 ns); PLL phase reference, NOT from the MS counter
signals.note: CORRECTED per §3.5/§3.8 (was mislabeled 'phase-2 of MC'). 02/ is ASYNC to the MS grid: 488 ns = 15 MS vs the 9-MS micro cycle, so its phase vs MS0 advances each cycle (realigns every 5 micros). The wave is ONE snapshot from fig-3.2: 02/ is LOW across MS0-MS3 and rises to HIGH at ~MS4 (held MS4-MS8). Because 02/ is async, that edge position drifts cycle-to-cycle; this is just the depicted phase, not a fixed MS relationship.

signals.name: MS0
signals.note: rises <17 ns into the cycle

signals.name: MS4
signals.note: clears AS0 (see AS1 note)

signals.name: MS7
signals.note: DAB WSTB (regfile write) is at MS7

signals.name: ARU CK
signals.note: pulse at each AS start; measured HIGH MS0.3-1.3 / MS3.3-4.3 / MS6.3-7.3 (rise lag ~0.3 MS, <19 ns; pulse spans into the next MS). The rising edges delimit the true ARU-state boundaries.

signals.name: AS0
signals.note: MEASURED signal (not the 'AS0=MS0-2' third): HIGH MS1-MS3. = the AS0 third shifted ~1 MS later by the clocked logic. Cleared by MS4 (rise lag <30 ns).

signals.name: AS1
signals.note: MEASURED signal: a NARROW pulse HIGH MS5-MS6 only (note: 'AS1 shorter than AS0/AS2 to avoid MS4 clearing AS0 prematurely'). Held off until MS5 so AS0's MS4-clear completes first.

signals.name: AS2
signals.note: MEASURED signal: HIGH MS7-MS8 and WRAPS the cycle boundary (cleared at MS1 of the next cycle), so MS0 shows the tail of the previous AS2. = the AS2 third shifted ~1 MS later. >46 ns after AS1.

figures.title: ARU module timing (MAC pipeline, deferred overlap)
figures.grid_note: ARU MAC pipeline across THREE microinstructions (i1,i2,i3); each AS = 3 MS = 97.66 ns. Owner-corrected placement 2026-06-27: operand read RA1/RA0 spans i1.AS1->i2.AS0; LOAD@i1.AS2 then SHIFT-right @i2.AS0 & i2.AS1; the operand weights (SHIFT REG), Booth coeff (M1,M0) and partial products all sit in i2.AS0-AS2; CSIGN valid i2.AS1->i3.AS0; the accumulate (0->±PP1->±(PP1+PP2)) runs i2.AS1->i3.AS0 (one AS behind the partial products = the pipeline register); ZERO/ and XFER CK pulse low at i2.AS0 (ZERO/ clears the accumulator so it reads 0 at i2.AS1; XFER CK lags ZERO/ ~20 ns). Shows the actions of one microinstruction's bits (pipelined/overlapped with neighbours).

signals.name: ARU CK
signals.note: one pulse per AS state

signals.name: RA1,RA0
signals.note: regfile read address valid i1.AS1 -> i2.AS0 (owner-read)

signals.name: S1,S0
signals.note: 74194 mode: LOAD@i1.AS2, SHIFT-right@i2.AS0 & i2.AS1 (owner-read)

signals.name: SHIFT REGISTER
signals.note: operand weight per AS, i2.AS0-AS2 (owner-corrected: shifted right to start at i2.AS0)

signals.name: M1
signals.note: odd Booth stream, i2.AS0-AS2. Chip = U10 (per T&C trace 060-02475 sh.2: U10 P0/P1/P2=C1//C3//C5/, /Q3=M1/).

signals.name: M0
signals.note: even Booth stream, i2.AS0-AS2. Chip = U11 (per T&C trace 060-02475 sh.2: U11 P0/P1/P2=C0//C2//C4/, /Q3=M0/).

signals.name: PARTIAL PRODUCT REG
signals.note: i2.AS0-AS2 (NOT explicitly owner-stated; kept one AS ahead of the accumulate = the pipeline register. CONFIRM)

signals.name: CSIGN
signals.note: valid i2.AS1 -> i3.AS0 (owner-read)

signals.name: ACCUMULATOR
signals.note: accumulate i2.AS1 -> i3.AS0 (owner-corrected: shifted right one cell). ZERO/ clears it at i2.AS0 so it reads 0 at i2.AS1

signals.name: ZERO/
signals.note: active-LOW: low pulse at i2.AS0 (clears accumulator), high again at i2.AS1 (owner-read)

signals.name: XFER CK
signals.note: active-LOW: same low pulse at i2.AS0 as ZERO/ but ~20 ns lag behind it (owner-read). Latches ACC -> result register

figures.title: DMEM timing (DRAM access strobes)
figures.grid_note: DMEM DRAM access within one microinstruction (MS grid). GRID PIXEL-CALIBRATED 2026-06-27: MS0 starts x=430, 81.1 px/MS, anchored to the printed MS-digit centers (MSO center=468). ALL signals owner-confirmed on this grid 2026-06-27 - the strobes (RAS//CAS//DAB WSTB//MEMW//DAB RSTB/RESET/), ROW SEL, and the OFST//ADDRESS/DOUT/DIN/CPC valid-windows (auto-extraction had been unreliable - annotation arrows cross the thin strobe traces - so they were read off by the owner). Per §3.6: DMEM strobes generated by delay-line U59 (DL630B) from T&C signals MEMAC, DABSTB, MEMR. Address = CPC - OFST (2's-complement: CPC + complemented OFST/ + carry-in tied HIGH; adder U49/U50/U63/U64), row/col-muxed onto the 8 DRAM address lines by U18/U36. CPC = 16-bit counter U51/U65, +1 per sample. RAM outputs drive the DAB directly (no buffer). Notes: 'CAS/ falls only when MEMAC is high.' 'Critical path for DIN is XFER CK -> ARU result register.'

signals.name: OFST/ VALID
signals.note: BUS valid-window (multi-bit, NO logic level). Held microinstruction-register value -> valid the whole cycle; only changes (reloads) at the MS0 boundary (<29ns settle). SAME shape every cycle; values differ. Used (latched by RAS/CAS) only when MEMAC. Lines feed adder B-inputs U49/U50/U63/U64 + read-back U48/U62.

signals.name: ADDRESS VALID
signals.note: BUS valid-window (multi-bit). = CPC-OFST, recomputed each cycle; valid ~MS2-MS8 after the <67ns settle at the boundary. SAME shape every cycle. Latched into the DRAMs by RAS/CAS only when MEMAC. = adder sums U49/U50/U63/U64 -> row/col mux U18/U36 -> 8 DRAM addr lines.

signals.name: RAS/
signals.note: active-LOW; row-address strobe; <44 ns

signals.name: ROW SEL
signals.note: OWNER-CONFIRMED on calibrated grid: HIGH (row addr) MS0-2, LOW (column addr) MS3-7, HIGH MS8 -> mux U18/U36 selects row then column for the DRAMs

signals.name: CAS/
signals.note: OWNER-CONFIRMED: active-LOW col-address strobe; HIGH MS0-3, falls@MS4, LOW MS4-8, rises@MS0 of next cycle (wraps). FALLS ONLY WHEN MEMAC HIGH

signals.name: DOUT VALID
signals.value_source: DRAM read data on the DAB
signals.note: BUS valid-window (multi-bit). Occurs ONLY on a READ cycle; DRAM read data is valid LATE (~MS7+, <85ns) after the access. = 4164 DOUT pins tied directly to the DAB. Model: valid at the DAB read strobe.

signals.name: DAB WSTB/
signals.note: READ: active-LOW; strobes read data onto DAB; <22 ns

signals.name: MEMW/
signals.note: WRITE: memory write enable; <83 ns. (Net is MEMW/ per the DMEM netlist 060-02512 U61 pin 11 -> DRAM WR/; the fig-3.3 'MFMW/' label was a transcription typo, owner-corrected.)

signals.name: DAB RSTB
signals.note: WRITE: latches DIN from DAB; <12 ns

signals.name: DIN VALID
signals.value_source: DRAM write data on the DAB (from ARU result reg)
signals.note: BUS valid-window (multi-bit). Occurs ONLY on a WRITE cycle. = 4164 DIN pins <- DAB, from the ARU result register (critical path = XFER CK -> result reg). Valid MS4-MS8 (owner-read); model only needs it valid at the write strobe.

signals.name: RESET/
signals.note: active-LOW; <21 ns, <35.5 ns

signals.name: CPC VALID (0-7)
signals.note: BUS valid-window (multi-bit; 16-bit counter U51/U65, row shows bits 0-7). Owner-read: VALID MS6 -> MS1 of the NEXT cycle (wraps), INVALID MS2-MS5. That 5-MS window = the <160.5 ns annotation. So MS0,MS1 = tail of the previous window; MS6,MS7,MS8 = new window.

figures.title: FPC float->fixed (A/D input) cycle
figures.grid_note: X-axis = INPUT CYCLE COUNTER 0-99 (one audio sample = 100 microinstructions × 293 ns; sample time 29.3 us). Per §3.8: ROM-generated by the 8-bit input-cycle counter U7/U8 -> 256x4 ROM U6 (only the first 100 locations used); some ROM outputs deglitched by register U18. Columns are decades; TRANSITIONS APPROXIMATE (snapped to ~10). Two channels convert on alternate halves (CONVERT CH2 ~0-38, CONVERT CH1 ~52-88). SELF-TEST (FPC DBUG floats high when T&C/ARU/DMEM pulled): muxes U4/U42 swap FPC CK->02/ and substitute RESET//RDAD//WRDA//SDAA-D from counter-decode NANDs U5. Notes: absent a RESET pulse all signals repeat beyond count 99; CH1L is generated on the AIN module, not FPC.

signals.name: FPCCK
signals.value_source: FPC CK, once per 293 ns system clock
signals.note: basic FPC clock from T&C

signals.name: RESET/
signals.note: active-LOW; syncs the timing cycle at counter 0 (WCS RESET at count 99)

signals.name: STBGN
signals.note: strobe-begin; APPROX edges (~38, ~52, ~88)

signals.name: STCONV/
signals.note: §3.8: brought HIGH at counter state 0 to START the AIN SAR conversion, held through the convert window, then back low (13th CNVCK resets SAR). [was inverted] APPROX edges

signals.name: CNV CK
signals.note: §3.8: 13 convert-clock pulses per conversion (SAR data valid after the 12th; 13th resets SAR). Bursts during convert windows

signals.name: LOAD
signals.note: §3.8: asserted for TWO clock pulses — 1st loads input-gain counter U16 (IGA1/IGA0), 2nd loads the 12-bit SAR data into shift regs U27/U28/U38/U39; then shift-left per gain (00->4 shifts,01->3,10->2,11->1)

signals.name: CH 1
signals.note: input channel select; CH1 ~0-52 then CH2

signals.name: CH1L
signals.note: §3.8/fig note: CH1 latched, generated on AIN (not FPC); its edge switches the input channel; APPROX

signals.name: DATA VALID
signals.value_source: conversion; gated to DAB by RD AD/
signals.note: §3.8: tri-state drivers U25/U26 gate converted data onto the DAB when the DSP asserts RD AD/. CH1 valid ~0-38, CH2 ~52-88 (gain-settling gaps)

signals.name: RDAD/ & WR DA/
signals.note: SELF-TEST MODE only (FPC DBUG high): pulses ~count 38 and ~63

signals.name: BIT5 of counter
signals.note: counter bit5 high for counts 32-63

figures.title: FPC fixed->float (D/A output) cycle
figures.grid_note: FPC D/A output sequencer (§3.8). Double buffer = 4-bit reg U40 (output-select SDAA-D) + 16-bit reg U36/U37 (output value); loaded on WR DA/, which sets NEW DAT/ (U3 pin6 = buffer full). When BUSY (from strobe counter U1/U2) is low, the value is loaded into shift reg U23/U24/U34/U35, the select into U41, and NEW DAT/ is cleared. Loading the shift reg also loads the strobe counter U1/U2 (initial 0x2A) and output-gain counter U43, starting an output cycle: gain counter counts up and the shift reg shifts LEFT until STOP/ (the two MSBs disagree = sign about to shift out) OR the counter has incremented 3 times. OGA1/OGA0 = 2 LSBs of the gain counter. flip-flop U3 then pulses mux-enable U42.15 to strobe OUT A-D. Coarse grid; TRANSITIONS APPROXIMATE.

signals.name: WR DA/
signals.note: active-LOW; DSP asserts to load the double buffer (a new output value)

signals.name: NEW DAT/
signals.note: asserted when buffer loaded; deasserted when value moves to the shift reg (gated by NOR U14.1 so a reload doesn't clear it early)

signals.name: BUSY
signals.note: high while a D/A conversion is running; inspected before loading the shift reg

signals.name: GCNT (U43)
signals.note: output-gain counter 0-3; =3 holds while SCNT runs; 2 LSBs -> OGA1/OGA0

signals.name: SCNT (U1/U2)
signals.note: strobe counter loaded at 0x2A, counts up during the conversion (293 ns/step)

signals.name: SHFT REG MODE (U23/24/34/35)
signals.note: shift-register mode for the float mantissa: LOAD, SHIFT-left (fixed->float), then NOP

signals.name: OUT (A/B/C/D)
signals.note: §3.8/§3.10: OUT goes high ~1.76 us after DAC+gain valid (settling), then valid ~4.08 us (fig labels 4.10)

signals.name: DAO-11,OGA0,OGA1 VALID
signals.note: 12-bit D/A data DA0-11 + output-gain bits OGA0/OGA1 valid window
